title: Receptionist
description:
Job Title: Remote Therapist Receptionist

Location: Remote (EST)

Employment Type: Full-Time

About The Role

Evolve Psychiatry is seeking a compassionate, organized, and customer-service-oriented Remote Therapist Receptionist to support our growing team of licensed therapists. This individual serves as the primary administrative point of contact for therapy patients, ensuring a seamless experience from initial inquiry through ongoing treatment.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, empathetic, comfortable working in a fast-paced healthcare environment, and able to manage multiple priorities while maintaining exceptional patient care. This role requires excellent communication skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently in a fully remote environment.

Responsibilities:

  • Answer phone calls and route inquiries appropriately
  • Schedule, confirm and modify appointments using our EMR system
  • Verify insurance information and collect copays
  • Communicate efficiently with therapists, patients, and other staff
  • Handle sensitive information with discretion and confidentiality

Qualifications:

  • Previous experience in a medical receptionist role working directly with therapists preferred
  • Experience in psychiatry or behavioral health
  • Proficient with electronic medical records and general computer skills
  • Strong organizational abilities and multitasking skills within a remote environment
  • Professional appearance/demeanor
  • Experience working in a fastpaced administrative environment
  • Strong computer proficiency

Success in This Role:The Ideal candidate is someone who:

  • Enjoys helping patients and providing outstanding customer service.
  • Can confidently manage a high volume of phone calls and appointment scheduling.
  • Thrives in a fastpaced, remote environment.
  • Pays close attention to detail and follows processes consistently.
  • Communicates professionally with patients, therapists, and other team members.
  • Takes initiative and is comfortable solving problems independently.

Why Join Us?

  • Be a part of a respected and growing psychiatry practice
  • Work in a collaborative, missiondriven environment
  • Competitive pay and benefits package
  • Make a meaningful difference in patients' lives everday

title: Analyst
description:
Who are we?

At Leadership Connect, we believe that relationships are everything in business and in life. Founded by two former CEOs of S&P 500 companies and a former presidential press secretary, our premier information service is built for developing relationships that impact decisions in government, business, and media. Utilizing deep research expertise and cutting-edge technology, we help our clients win business and influence policy across various specialties and verticals. We are a close-knit team dedicated to helping each of our users make a difference.

Leadership Connect Canada (LCC), a subsidiary of Leadership Connect Inc., is our Canadian entity operating in the province of Quebec.

About The Role

Working within our Data Team, we are seeking an Analyst to conduct and perform research and analysis tasks to develop and maintain our core data assets, people intelligence. The incumbent will actively participate in the development of our data operations activities while ensuring the accuracy and quality of our people intelligence data product. This role is ideal for data enthusiasts and problem solvers, keen on exercising ownership with a passion for empowering users and customers with information solutions that drive sales enablement and improve the collaboration between our customers and the people who are mission-critical to their organization. We embrace a mindset of continuous learning, and if youâre passionate about data collection, analysis, and development, we want to hear from you!

Core Functions Of The Role

  • Execute comprehensive data collection, development, and analysis supporting operational processes with a focus on relevance, precision, and accuracy
  • Undertaking in-depth analysis into key entities within our People Intelligence data product ensuring current and accurate data
  • Employ data analysis techniques to discern patterns, derive insights, and identify data quality discrepancies.
  • Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams to appraise data quality or to develop workflows contributing to enhancements in data completeness
  • Perform in an agile environment, in an operational context while facilitating subject matter interactions between interdisciplinary teams

About You

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience with an interest in people intelligence
  • 1-2 years of experience in data analysis and statistical research
  • Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
  • Strong organizational and project management skills
  • Strong time management practices and a proven ability to meet deadlines
  • Ability to communicate across teams
  • Proficiency in conducting sophisticated web-based research
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel or other data management systems
  • Practical knowledge of data analytics tools like PowerBI, QuickSight or others is a plus

$40,000 - $55,000 a year

To attract and engage high-level professionals, it is crucial to showcase our expertise, experience, and commitment to fostering valuable connections. We understand the importance of transparency when it comes to salary matters. Our research and analysis of the Quebec market serve as the foundation for determining appropriate salary ranges. However, we also recognize that factors like location, experience, skills alignment, and other compensation considerations may influence these ranges. At Leadership Connect, we place a strong emphasis on open communication and adaptability to ensure fair and competitive compensation. We invite talented individuals to join our team and help us create a workforce that is motivated and empowered to achieve new levels of success.

What Is It Like to Work Here?

We foster an environment that encourages all our team members to excel by offering competitive compensation for top talent. We believe in creating a friendly and enjoyable workplace, with regular team-building events, happy hours, and more. Transparency is key within our company, and we hold monthly town halls led by our CEO to address questions regarding business plans, product direction, and company goals. In addition, we provide flexible PTO policies to ensure you can enjoy your time outside the office and focus on your personal life.

Are you ready to join our dynamic team and build meaningful relationships that make a difference? Apply today!

Benefits/Rewards

  • Awesome Extended Health Care Plan
  • Dental Care
  • Life & Disability insurance
  • Health spending accounts
  • Unlimited Vacation Leave
  • 12 Paid Holidays
  • $3,000 Employee Referral Program
  • Employer contribution to VRSP
  • Rewards and recognition programs

If youâre excited about this role but donât meet 100% of the requirements, we still want to hear from you!

Leadership Connect is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce where unique experiences are valued and everyone has the opportunity to contribute. Research has continuously shown that women and people of color are less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet all of the listed qualifications. We want to help overcome this trend and seek to make space for unique and relevant skills and attributes. So, when applying to Leadership Connect we focus on evaluation based on key competencies needed for success in the position. We look forward to receiving your application!

Leadership Connect is committed to creating a diverse environment and is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Head of People
description:
About Bizee

Bizee has helped nearly two million entrepreneurs start and run their businesses. Now we are building what comes next: an AI-powered Entrepreneur Operating System that helps founders launch, operate, stay compliant, and grow with less friction and more confidence.

We are profitable, founder-led, and operating at a clear inflection point. The next chapter requires a stronger operating system for how we hire, lead, communicate, develop talent, and scale execution across the company.

The Opportunity

We are looking for a Head of People & Organizational Effectiveness to serve as a strategic partner to the President and executive team.

This is not a maintenance HR role. It is a builder-operator role for someone who can create clarity, raise the talent bar, strengthen the leadership layer, and build the people systems that help Bizee scale without unnecessary complexity or organizational drag.

As Bizee grows and transforms, one of the most important responsibilities of this role will be building and protecting a high talent bar. We are not simply adding headcount; we are intentionally increasing talent density across the company. This leader will help ensure we hire high-caliber builders, operators, and leaders who raise the standard, strengthen execution, and fit the culture we are building.

You will help design how Bizee hires, onboards, develops, manages, communicates, and performs as we move into our next stage of growth.

What You Will Own

  • Partner with the President and executive team on org design, leadership effectiveness, talent quality, succession, performance, and cross-functional friction
  • Design and protect Bizee's talent bar. Build the hiring standards, role scorecards, interview calibration, decision rules, and leadership alignment required to consistently identify high-caliber talent as the company scales. Ensure we do not compromise on quality-of-hire in the name of speed
  • Design a scalable hiring system, including workforce planning, role definition, scorecards, interview loops, calibration, decision frameworks, and candidate experience
  • Build Bizee's People Operating System, including the rhythms, mechanisms, and rituals that drive alignment, accountability, and performance
  • Strengthen the manager layer through practical enablement, feedback systems, performance routines, and leadership expectations
  • Design and own Bizee's performance cycle: goal setting, ratings, calibration, and linkage to comp and promotion decisions
  • Advise on role clarity, leveling, compensation philosophy, incentive design, spans, layers, and capability planning
  • Build onboarding, engagement, and internal communication mechanisms that help employees ramp faster and stay connected to the mission
  • Lead change management as Bizee modernizes its platform, operating model, AI-enabled workflows, and distributed teams
  • Plan and execute the eventual transition away from full co-employment (PEO) arrangements in multiple countries globally, including building the internal HRIS, payroll, benefits and compliance infrastructure needed to stand up direct employment, sequencing the migration to minimize risk and disruption, and managing the associated legal, tax, and employee communication considerations.
  • Own multi-state wage and hour compliance and workforce administration for a distributed employee population spanning nearly all 50 U.S. states, encompassing both exempt and non-exempt staff
  • Lead and integrate teams across multiple countries internationally, ensuring a single coherent Bizee culture and set of operating standards translate effectively across geographies, management structures, and local labor norms

Requirements

What You Will Bring

  • 10+ years of progressive experience in People, Talent, HRBP, Organizational Effectiveness, or People Operations leadership
  • Experience building People systems from the ground up, ideally in a high-growth, profitable, founder-led, PE-backed, SaaS, platform, marketplace, or tech-enabled services environment
  • Strong executive judgment and the ability to operate as a true strategic partner to a President and executive team
  • Proven experience with hiring systems, talent calibration, performance management, manager enablement, org design, leveling, compensation frameworks, and People Ops infrastructure
  • A strong point of view on talent density, quality-of-hire, leadership standards, and what it takes to build a high-performance company
  • Strong operator instincts: structured, practical, clear, and biased toward action
  • High EQ and high backbone: able to coach leaders, challenge assumptions, handle sensitive issues, and create clarity without adding drama or bureaucracy
  • Strategic and hands-on: able to advise the executive team in the morning and fix an onboarding workflow in the afternoon
  • Business-first orientation: understands revenue, margin, productivity, execution, and the tradeoffs of scaling a profitable company
  • Low-drama, high-signal communication style: brings facts, judgment, candor, and calm to complex situations
  • Culture carrier: protects what makes Bizee special while raising the performance bar
  • Comfort operating in ambiguity and building while the business is moving fast
  • Strong written communication skills and the ability to turn messy organizational issues into simple plans and operating mechanisms
  • Demonstrated experience managing a multi-state U.S. workforce (ideally 40+ states) that includes both exempt and non-exempt employees, with working knowledge of state-by-state wage and hour variation and how to operationalize compliance without adding unnecessary drag
  • Experience leading or partnering with teams in multiple countries, with a track record of integrating culture, standards, and management practices across borders rather than running parallel, disconnected functions
  • Direct experience transitioning a workforce out of a co-employment/PEO model, including standing up in-country or in-state direct employment infrastructure (payroll, benefits, statutory compliance) and managing the change process for affected employees and managers

What Success Looks Like

  • In the first year, Bizee has a stronger People Operating System that improves alignment, ownership, speed, and accountability across the company
  • Hiring is more structured, faster, and higher-signal. Leaders know what great looks like by role and level, hiring decisions are more disciplined, and the company is increasing talent density as it scales
  • Managers are better equipped to set direction, coach performance, handle conflict, and create clarity. Employees have stronger expectations, better communication, and a clearer understanding of how their work connects to the company's mission

The Head of People & Organizational Effectiveness becomes a trusted advisor to the President, executive team, and leadership layer, helping Bizee scale with discipline, clarity, high standards, and good judgment.

Benefits

Disclaimer:

Bizee is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Bizee is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

The compensation offered to the successful applicant may vary based on factors including experience, skills, education, location, and other job-related reasons.

This employer participates in E-verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S.

title: Backend Developer
description:
albo

albo is a leading fintech company offering financial products to individuals and SMBs with the mission to bring financial freedom to everyone everywhere.

About The Role

As a Senior Backend Engineer you'll design, build, and operate the microservices that power albo's core financial products â from card issuing and authorization to credit lines, payments, and money movement. You'll own services end-to-end across our polyglot, event-driven platform, making pragmatic architectural decisions that keep money-critical flows correct, idempotent, and observable at scale. You'll work close to the domain (SPEI, ledgers, credit, authorization) and close to production, partnering with Product, Infra, and other engineering squads to ship reliable financial infrastructure.

What Youâll Be Doing At Albo

  • Design, build, and maintain backend microservices in Go and Java/Quarkus within our domain-oriented architecture (customer, account, transactional, and shared domains).
  • Own services end-to-end: architecture, implementation, testing, deployment to QA/SBX/PROD, monitoring, and on-call ownership of what you ship.
  • Build event-driven and REST integrations across services (Pub/Sub, service-to-service REST via our private-proxy mesh), with strong guarantees around idempotency, reconciliation, and failure handling (dead-letter/reprocess, no silent data loss).
  • Model and evolve data across MongoDB and SingleStore/MemSQL, keeping dual-write flows consistent between the display source of truth and what card authorization actually validates.
  • Ship through our CI/CD (Jenkins shared pipelines, containerized deploys to GKE/Kubernetes on GCP), managing configuration and secrets via GCP Secret Manager.
  • Write clean, self-documenting, well-tested code; participate in code reviews and raise the engineering bar for correctness and simplicity.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Infra/Platform, and other backend squads to break down epics into shippable, incremental deliveries.
  • Instrument observability (structured logging, metrics, tracing) and use it to debug production issues and continuously improve reliability and performance.
  • Contribute to technical design docs, architecture diagrams, and shared conventions/scaffolding that scale the team.

About You And What Type Of Skills You'll Need

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field (or equivalent hands-on experience).
  • +5 years of experience building and operating backend services in production, ideally in the Banking or Fintech industry.
  • Strong proficiency in at least one of Go or Java (Quarkus/Spring or similar), and willingness to work across both in a polyglot environment.
  • Solid experience with microservices and distributed systems: REST APIs, asynchronous/event-driven messaging (Pub/Sub, Kafka, or similar), service boundaries, and versioned contracts.
  • Hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred), containers and Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
  • Comfort with both SQL and NoSQL data stores (MongoDB, SingleStore/MySQL/Postgres) and an eye for data consistency in money-critical flows.
  • Deep understanding of reliability patterns â idempotency, retries, reconciliation, graceful degradation â and why they matter when handling real money.
  • Familiarity with fintech/payments concepts (card authorization, ledgers, transfers, credit) is a strong plus.
  • Customer Centricity: You're customer-obsessed, constantly thinking about improving the customer experience.
  • Hands-on: We're looking for a comfortable and willing leader to be a hands-on contributor who is energized by rolling up their sleeves, making things happen, and enabling the team to do the same.
  • Done is better than perfect: As things change and move quickly, you are excited by the fast pace and opportunity to constantly learn and help your team learn.
  • Communication: Excellent ability to communicate internally and externally, quickly build relationships, and work cross-functionally.
  • Extreme ownership: You hold yourself accountable to a high bar. You are supremely organized and see what needs to happen to achieve goals.
  • Results & Data Driven: You understand the business metrics you are responsible for, and you demonstrate these insights to drive constant improvement.

The Klick Groupâcomprising Klick Health (including Klick Katalyst and btwelve), Klick Media Group, Klick Applied Sciences (including Klick Labs), Klick Consulting, and Klick Venturesâis an ecosystem dedicated to realizing the full potential of our people and clients in life sciences. Consistently named a Best Managed Company, Most Admired Corporate Culture, and Great Place to Work, Klick is committed to fostering a high-performance, people-first culture with creativity, collaboration, innovation, and impact across everything we do.

About Our Creative Craft

Our Creative Craft brings Klickâs ideas to life through storytelling, design, and experience. This craft blends imagination with strategy to create work that is emotionally resonant and culturally relevant. Creative teams collaborate across disciplines to simplify complexity and humanize healthcare. Their work drives engagement, understanding, and meaningful action across channels.

Job Description

As a key member of the Creative Operations and Resourcing group, you will report to the Senior Creative Resource Manager and play a vital role in keeping processes on track. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys administrative work, has strong communication and organizational skills, and wants to contribute to a dynamic creative environment.

Additional Information

The hourly range for this position is between CAD $40/hour and CAD $45/hour. The final amount offered will be determined based on several factors such as a candidate's work location, their unique skill set, education, and prior work experience.

Our Commitment to Inclusion

Klick is consciously creating a culture where everyone can thrive and grow in their careers. We believe that our best work comes from our diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. We strongly encourage members of historically underrepresented communities to apply, including Black people, Indigenous peoples, and other people of color, people with disabilities, people from gender and sexually diverse communities and people with intersectional identities. We're also committed to developing an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment process and work environment. Should you require any accommodation, please contact us at careers@klick.com and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs and ensure you have a positive experience.

title: Junior Operations Support Specialist
description:
Guesty is the all-in-one platform helping hospitality businesses around the world automate, optimize, and scale their operations. Weâre a fast-growing global startup thatâs transforming how the industry works â from guest experience to business growth.

With a powerful suite of features and integrations with 150+ industry partners â including Airbnb, Vrbo, Booking.com, Expedia, Google Travel, and many more â Guesty empowers property managers to deliver top-tier guest experiences while running more efficient, data-driven operations.

Weâre proud to have over 900+ team members across 16 countries worldwide, all working together to build the future of hospitality technology.

If youâre looking to grow your career in a dynamic, global, and impact-driven environment, weâd love to hear from you.

Weâre looking for a detail-oriented and service-minded Junior Sales Operations Support Specialist to join our Sales Operations team.

This role is focused on providing day-to-day operational support to the Sales team, ensuring their tools, data, and processes run smoothly. Youâll play a hands-on role in unblocking sales representatives, maintaining data quality, and helping keep our sales systems clean and efficient.

This is an ideal opportunity for someone early in their Sales Operations or Revenue Operations career who is eager to learn the ins and outs of sales processes, tools, and cross-functional collaboration.

Only resumes submitted in English will be reviewed.

Responsibilities:

  • Frontline Sales Support: Youâll serve as a go-to resource for resolving operational blockers that slow down the sales process â from fixing record errors to assisting with quote approvals and troubleshooting common issues.
  • Systems & Tooling Assistance: Help monitor and maintain the health of our sales tech stack. This includes addressing basic system errors, supporting user questions, and escalating more complex issues as needed.
  • Data Integrity & Maintenance: Contribute to the accuracy of our CRM and reporting by identifying and correcting data inconsistencies, duplicates, and misalignments between contracts, opportunities, and records.
  • Reporting & Process Support: Assist with building or updating reports and dashboards for the sales team and reviewing existing ones to catch errors or outdated information.
  • Operational Monitoring: Support routine checks and reviews of sales data and performance indicators to ensure nothing falls through the cracks â like unassigned deals, stalled opportunities, or misrouted accounts.

Requirements:

  • 1+ year of experience in a Sales, Sales Ops, RevOps, or CRM support role (internships count!)
  • Experience using Salesforce or another CRM platform is preferred.
  • Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills, including confidence working with large datasets.
  • Detail-oriented and organized with a strong sense of ownership
  • Curious and eager to learn new systems and sales operations best practices
  • Ability to manage multiple requests and prioritize urgent issues.
  • Comfortable communicating with stakeholders and asking clarifying questions
  • Ability to work in a fast-moving environment with changing priorities.
  • Flexible and comfortable in a fast-moving environment with shifting priorities
  • Interested in a long-term path in Sales or Revenue Operations

Nice to Have

  • Experience building Salesforce reports and dashboards.
  • Familiarity with AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude or similar technologies.
  • Experience working with ticketing or internal support systems.
  • Understanding of common Sales processes, including leads, accounts, opportunities, ownership, and approvals.
  • Experience working in a SaaS or technology company.

Guesty is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants regardless of race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, disability, veteran status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: General Labour
description:
Come Build Your Career at Aecon!

Aecon delivers some of the most complex and impactful infrastructure projects â from transformative transportation networks to critical energy, industrial and nuclear programs. The projects we build connect communities and power future generations. With deep roots in North America and a strong footprint internationally, Aecon brings global expertise and proudly serves public and private sector clients through its Construction and Concessions segment.

Safety Always is not only our #1 core value â it is the standard that anchors our culture. We believe the most ambitious projects deserve the most committed people. At Aecon, you wonât just build your career â youâll help build what matters to enable future generations to thrive.

At Aecon, You Can Count On

  • Safety Always. Our number one core value. The safety of our people, projects, partners, and stakeholders is our priority focus â today and always.
  • Integrity. We lead by example, with humility and courage.
  • Accountability. Weâre passionate about delivering on our commitments.
  • Inclusion. We provide opportunities where people feel valued, supported, and empowered to contribute fully.


We deliver infrastructure with purpose, and our people are at the heart of everything we do. Aecon employees are incredibly proud to build some of the most impactful infrastructure of this generation â we call it Aecon Proud.

At Aecon We

  • Ensure you and your family receive the services and benefits needed to support your mental, emotional, and physical well-being.
  • Are intentional when it comes to investing in your development. We help you build your career and advance your skills through our Aecon University, tuition reimbursement, and Leadership Programs.
  • Are committed to creating work environments focused on mutual respect, teamwork, collaboration, and new ideas, through meaningful initiatives, training, partnerships with Veteran groups, our Aecon Women in Trades and diversity programs, as well as our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), to ensure we are building with top talent and harnessing our collective strengths within every aspect of our culture.
  • Operate responsibly by managing risk, safety, and environmental considerations across all our projects and surrounding communities.


Our success is built on the passion, expertise, and dedication of our people. Together, we embrace strong execution, innovation, and continuous improvement â values that come to life through the unique talents and collaborative spirit of every team member. If youâre inspired to make a difference through future-building projects, join our best-in-class team.

What is the Opportunity?

Aeconâs Civil East sector is a cornerstone of our construction and infrastructure operations, delivering innovative solutions to some of the most complex and impactful projects globally. Renowned for its expertise in heavy civil construction, our Civil East sector specializes in building essential infrastructure that powers economies and enhances communities.

In the Caribbean region, we are leveraging our expertise and collaborative approach to deliver world-class infrastructure projects tailored to meet the unique needs of the island communities.

We are looking for a General Labourer to support and assist the team in building construction, especially with set up, clean up and ensuring a safe working environment.

What Youâll Do Here

  • Entry level position, we are willing to train the right candidate - must be able to follow instructions, have a strong work ethic, reliable, and self-motivated.
  • Ability to lift 50 lbs. on a regular basis and stand/walk for long periods of time.
  • Ability to communicate with supervisors, co-workers, contractors and the general public in a courteous, professional manner at all times.
  • Prepare and clean up construction sites.
  • Move materials and equipment and perform demolition, excavation and compaction activities.
  • Handle and distribute construction materials (e.g. load and unload vehicles with supplies, equipment and construction materials; move tools, equipment and construction materials to and from work areas; remove rubble and other debris).
  • Spot for equipment operators when required.
  • General site maintenance.


What You Bring To The Team

  • Physical strength and stamina
  • Manual dexterity
  • A safety-conscious attitude
  • The ability to work with others in a team


Aecon fosters belonging within and across our organization. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and considering all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws.

title: Finance & HR Specialist
description:
Job Summary:

The Finance & HR Specialist serves as a key member of the Business Operations team, owning the day-to-day financial, payroll, and people operations functions of the organization. This role is responsible for ensuring accuracy, organization, and efficiency across invoicing, billing quality control, employee and contractor administration, payroll support, and HR operations. This individual partners closely with external partners to ensure smooth financial operations while supporting the employee and contractor lifecycle from onboarding through offboarding.


Duties/Responsibilities:

Financial Operations

  • Own the invoicing and billing quality control process to ensure accuracy and completeness prior to invoice submission
  • Review timesheets, bill rates, pay rates, markups, expenses, and billing data for accuracy
  • Coordinate invoice corrections and resolve billing discrepancies with internal teams
  • Serve as the primary operational liaison with the Companyâs external accounting and finance partners
  • Support payroll processing activities and payroll audits
  • Assist with financial reporting requests
  • Maintain financial records, reports, and supporting documentation
  • Process and track employee and contractor expense reimbursements
  • Support workersâ compensation audits, insurance audits, and client audit requests
  • Facilitate and own accounts receivable follow-up and collections activities

Payroll Administration

  • Support payroll processing activities and payroll quality control reviews
  • Ensure accurate collection and validation of timekeeping records
  • Process payroll changes including new hires, terminations, compensation changes, deductions, and garnishments
  • Serve as the point of contact with payroll-related inquiries from employees and contractors
  • Support the implementation and administration of an internal payroll function

HR Operations and Program Support

  • Coordinate employee and contractor onboarding and offboarding activities
  • Administer background checks, employment verification requests, and onboarding documentation
  • Maintain employee personnel files and HR documentation in compliance with company policies and applicable laws
  • Administer and coordinate benefits administration activities including enrollments, changes, and qualifying life events
  • Support open enrollment activities and communications
  • Track policy acknowledgements, required training, and compliance requirements
  • Assist with unemployment claims administration and workersâ compensation documentation
  • Maintain HRIS data integrity and employee records
  • Support the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of HR programs, policies, and processes
  • Coordinate employee engagement initiatives, recognition programs, and culture initiatives
  • Support special projects and strategic initiatives as assigned by leadership
  • Perform other duties as assigned


Qualifications/Requirements:

  • Associateâs or Bachelorâs degree in Business Administration, Human Resources, Accounting, Finance, or a related field preferred
  • 3+ years of experience in accounting administration, payroll administration, human resources administration, business operations, or a related role
  • Experience owning and supporting invoicing, billing, payroll, accounts payable, accounts receivable, or financial administration activities
  • Experience supporting employee onboarding, benefits administration, personnel records management, and other HR administrative functions
  • Experience working with HRIS, payroll, accounting, or ERP systems.
  • Experience working with QuickBooks
  • Experience supporting multi-state employees and contractors preferred
  • Experience within staffing, professional services, consulting, or project-based organizations preferred


Knowledge, Skills & Abilities:

  • Ability to work independently and in a team environment
  • Excellent organizational and project management skills
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Ability to prioritize multiple competing deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Knowledge of payroll processes and practices
  • Knowledge of general accounting principles and financial administrative practices
  • Ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise sound judgement
  • Strong customer service and relationship management skills
  • Ability to identify discrepancies and resolve issues proactively
  • Ability to document and improve processes and workflows
  • Ability to establish priorities and demonstrate a strong sense of urgency
  • Ability to adapt to changing business priorities and organizational needs
  • Ability to manage multiple projects and priorities simultaneously while meeting deadlines
  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and reporting tools


Benefits:

  • Dental insurance
  • Health insurance
  • Health savings account
  • Life insurance
  • Paid time off
  • Retirement plan
  • Vision insurance

About us:

Grail Talent is an Influencer Management Agency that connects our diverse and carefully curated roster of content creators with digital marketing opportunities, working with brands and record labels to advertise products and music on social media through influencer promotion and performance marketing.

We work with some of the biggest players in Music and New Media, including Sony Music Entertainment and Amazon Prime Video, to help promote the next viral sensation or hit show. In doing so, we are shaping the future of how digital marketing budgets are spent; delivering dynamic and creative promotional campaigns across a variety of social media channels such as TikTok and Instagram, and measuring their impact with detailed analytics.

As an Influencer Talent Manager, you will be responsible for managing the careers of our creators, helping them grow their audience and monetise their content. Using our in-house software and your own expertise, you will also be responsible for negotiating deals with brands/record labels and developing content strategies for our clients.

title: Social Media Content Creator
description:
Company Description


Here's the problem we're obsessed with: when you want to do something in your free time, you open TikTok or you ask ChatGPT and both send you to the exact same place as everyone else. The same viral pasta spot, the same rooftop, the same top-10 list. The algorithm flattens a whole city into five places and funnels everyone into them.


But there's no single Milan! There are as many as there are pairs of eyes looking at it, and yours looks nothing like the top-10 everyone else gets fed. Hic finds your version of the city and lets you live it in one tap. We're live in Milan, Florence, Berlin, London, Madrid, and Zurich.


Our content fights the same fight: not "5 things to do in Milan" (that's the enemy), but the specific, the hidden, the strange. Restaurants worth crossing town for, museums that aren't boring, bars, corners, and the stories nobody told you. The stuff that makes someone feel like the city was built for them. That's what you'd be making.




Role Description


We don't want to hand you a template. We want someone to own our TikTok voice and run with it: your ideas, your style, your hooks. You'll have the creative freedom to make people fall in love with Hic, however you see it, working hand in hand with the founder. You'd make around 4 short videos a week, with room to grow: shoot Milan on the ground, and edit footage for the other cities. This is paid freelance, project-based to start, part-time. A young app whose whole voice you get to shape. The only rule: it's about real places people can actually go. What it's not: a corporate content job or an unpaid "exposure" gig.




Qualifications


  • You are original: (you bring ideas, you've got taste and a point of view).
  • You live on TikTok and it shows (you get hooks and retention in your bones).
  • You have an account that actually posts.
  • Fast in CapCut (or whatever gets it done).
  • Based in Milan, and you can go shoot on location.
  • You own the creative, but you're happy to shape strategy together with the team.




If you are interested DM us your TikTok account or one you manage!

About the role:

The US F&B Head of Marketing is entrusted with shaping and elevating CHANELâs Brand Equity in the US, for the three strategic categories - Fragrance, Skincare and Makeup, in line with Global strategies and local market realities. This visionary leader can connect modernity with heritage, promote Brand saliency and desirability while helping drive data-informed, innovative and clientâcentric growth, in a marketing landscape shaped by AI, innovation and new possibilities. Our ideal candidate is attuned with the ever-changing rules of the US market and client journeys and energized by fostering deep emotional connections between the Brand, its existing and potential clients.

This position orchestrates the full marketing ecosystem with a strategic mindset, inspiring and promoting a collaborative partnership with DCX, Brand Communication and Artistic Direction teams, locally and globally. Leading both Brand Management and Trade & Retail Marketing teams, this role ensures that every expression of the brand is coherent, elevated and culturally resonant, creating ultimate client experience at every touchpoint.

What impact you can create at CHANEL:

Brand Strategy and Brand Equity Stewardship


  • Champion Chanelâs brand equity, ensuring all initiatives reflect the Houseâs heritage, creative vision, and uncompromising standards.
  • Translate global brand strategies into locally relevant, highâimpact plans that reinforce CHANELâs local leadership in luxury fragrance and beauty, while in return promoting US market and client realities towards Global teams, to ensure that these specific needs are understood and translated into product and orchestration strategies.
  • Be the voice of the Brand to communicate CHANELâs vision and support priorities to our Retailers and Market Teams, inspiring alignment and ambition.


Category Expertise Across the Three Axis


  • Demonstrate deep strategic and operational mastery of Fragrance, Skincare, and Makeup, understanding category dynamics, competitive landscapes, and client evolving motivations, to orchestrate locally relevant activations and nourish Global MTP product plans.
  • Guide category teams to build longâterm equity while delivering shortâterm business performance, supervising and consolidating A&P budget by axis, facilitating prioritization and monitoring sales performance and ROI to adapt plans and investments in real time.
  • Commission and interpret marketing intelligence, consumer research, competitive analysis, and trend forecasting to inform strategy. Translate customer data and market insights into articulated action plans by axis, govern implementation and measure success, with the support of the Transformation Office, Insights, Brand Comm (BC) and DCX teams. Connect the dots and lead the evolution from episodic insights to AI-augmented intuition, sensing cultural shifts and market opportunities.


360Â° Marketing Orchestration and Execution


  • Conduct the orchestra, leading the endâtoâend development and implementation of integrated marketing campaigns, partnering closely with Brand Communication, Digital and Client Experience, Artistic Department and Retail teams, as well as Global partners.
  • Beyond campaigns, ensure â with the support of the DCX and BC teams â that we create relevant and always-on connection across touchpoints of the clientâs journeys (events, digital activations, retail experiences, servicesâ¦), to convey the Houseâs storytelling through elevated and innovative multiâchannel expressions, elevating client lifetime value.
  • Lead in a fluid world: operate in a composable marketing model where teams and skills reconfigure around outcomes, not just fixed roles.
  • Client Journey & Experience Expertise
  • Bring a sophisticated understanding of modern F&B client journeys across online and offline ecosystems for each product axis.
  • Partner with DCX and Retail teams to ensure that every online and offline interactionâ from digital discovery, in-store experience, events or postâpurchase engagement â feels seamless and reflects the Houseâs signature level of service.
  • Create unforgettable moments: together with the Artistic and Education teams, support the Retail to deliver extraordinary in-store and event experiences, consistent with Brandâs standards.
  • Leverage insights and data to anticipate the expectations of the diverse US clientele (esp younger targets) and shape meaningful, personalized experiences for launches and core product offer.


Leadership & Team Development


  • Inspire, mentor, and develop highâperforming teams, nurturing curiosity and innovative spirit, accountability, and crossâfunctional collaboration.
  • Foster a culture of operational excellence aligned with Chanelâs image and values, where team members also feel empowered to bring âout of the boxâ ideas to create Brand magic and unique story-telling.
  • Lead with agility and promote simplification: guide an evolving organization, where roles continuously reconfigure as AIâs impact rises. Continuously assess what to automate, augment or eliminate, focusing teams on Brand priorities and high-value work. Partner with P&O to pilot adaptive organization principles when relevant.


Strategic Partnership & Influence


  • Develop strong relationships and trust with leaders of the rest of the marketing ecosystem (DCX, AD, Brand Comm) and collaborate with global and regional stakeholders to ensure strategic alignment and bestâinâclass execution.
  • Represent the voice of the market: provide sharp insights to influence global strategy and innovation pipelines.


You are energized by:


  • Developing and executing strategic plans
  • Setting lofty goals and achieving them
  • Working in a highly collaborative environment
  • Navigating complexity and supporting consistent transformation and change focusing on efficiency
  • Collaborating with passionate teammates
  • People leadership, coaching and development
  • Creative problem solving and learning something new every day


What you will bring to the team:


  • Product sensibility across Fragrance, Makeup and Skincare with deep fluency in F&B brand equity building in a US competitive and diverse environment.
  • Visionary and strategic thinker with strong executional discipline, able to think out of the box, bring the outside in and challenge conventions while honoring brand heritage and delivering exceptional client experience.
  • Inspirational leader who attracts, develops and retains top talents.
  • Deep cultural intuition and trend curiosity.
  • Data-driven and able to transform insights into action plans
  • Holistic expertise of the modern omni-channel client journey and the psychology of premium US consumers across age pyramid, as well as understanding of client-centric communication automation tools and growing impact of digital and AI on Marketing function.
  • Strong financial acumen and budget management skills.
  • Comfortable to lead by influence in complex environments, rely on centers of expertise (Brand Comm and DCX, Global teams) while challenging and connecting the dots.


Position Logistics:


  • Partially Remote: Role requires a minimum of three days in-person office presence at NYC office
  • Minimum of 20 years of experience and 15+ years of specific marketing experience
  • Strong analytical skills and the ability to synthesize data easily
  • Experience leading teams and leaders of teams
  • Bachelorâs degree required, Masterâs degree or MBA preferred
  • Proficient use of Microsoft 365 programs is required
  • Ability to travel internationally and domestically approximately 15% -20% or as business requires
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication required with an understanding of French language preferable


Compensation:


  • The anticipated base salary range for this position is $256,100 through $400,000. Base salary is one component of the total compensation for this position. Other forms of variable pay [may/will] be offered for this position. Other components [may/will] include bonus potential, benefits, and/or perks.


Benefits and Perks:


  • Wellbeing resources include dedicated paid time off for wellbeing (2-week August Office Closure) and a Wellbeing fund
  • Family and care giving benefits (inclusive of parental leave, fertility support, MilkStork, and Care.com Membership)
  • Generous paid time off policies to include vacation, holiday, sick and volunteer days
  • 401K and other incentives
  • Robust healthcare offerings; medical, dental, vision, MDLIVE (virtual care), One Medical, Flexible Spending Accounts (Health Care & Dependent Care), Health Savings Account and Employee Assistance Program
  • Life insurance, Accidental Death & Dismemberment, Short Term Disability, Long Term Disability, Health Advocate, International Business Travel Accident & Medical, and Commuter Transit & Parking


Additional Information:

Chanel is a private company whose values are grounded in creating the conditions for people to perform at their best and feel fulfilled and confident in their work. We offer a unique work environment where individuals are encouraged to better understand the brand, the business, and motivations, so that together we can unlock the possibilities of growth. This is reflected in:

Diversity and Inclusion:


  • At CHANEL, we are intentional in promoting Diversity & Inclusion. We foster respect, empathy and dignity for all. We believe strongly that the diversity of our people across the full spectrum of human differences is essential to our organisation and the connections we have with each other and our clients.
  • We offer Employee Resource Groups in the US that are voluntary, open to all, employee-led groups formed around a shared identity or lived experience, whose aim is to foster a diverse, inclusive, and equitable community aligned with the values and missions of the communities they support.


Chanel Community:


  • CHANEL Community empowers our employees to channel their passions, talents and sense of purpose to contribute to and learn from our communities.
  • Employees are encouraged to take time off annually to volunteer through CHANEL Community. CHANEL also matches employee donations to select charitable organizations.


Sustainability:


  • CHANEL Mission 1.5Â° is our climate action plan. It is focused on transforming the business in line with the ambitions of the Paris Agreement on climate change to limit the average global mean temperature increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius above preindustrial levels.
  • Employees are encouraged to contribute to our US Sustainability efforts at the corporate level and within the divisions, through organized taskforces and initiatives.


Arts and Culture:


  • We are committed to extending our legacy of cultural engagement. Global support includes the CHANEL Culture Fund, which supports a select group of leading art culture institutions across the globe, and the CHANEL Next Prize for the next generation of creative talent. US support currently includes Annual sponsorship of the MoMA film program, Tribeca Film Festival programs and Through Her Lens, Support of the Academy Gold Fellowship for Women and Sponsorship of the BAAND Together Dance Festival at Lincoln Center.


Fondation Chanel:


  • Since 2011, Fondation CHANELâs mission is for women and girls to be free to shape their own destiny. Through multi-year partnerships with financial and technical support, Fondation CHANEL is committed to improving the safety and autonomy of women and adolescent girls around the world, impacting over a million women and girls in its first 10 years.
  • For more information, please navigate to the Fondation Chanel website here


Career and Leadership Development:


  • We have dedicated in-house teams focused on supporting the onboarding of employees, developing leadership skills via custom programs like Imagine Chanel People, Heart of Leadership and group and individual coaching, and blended online and live classes offered on our Bloom platform and by skilled trainers, such as Shape Your Career, to develop career building skills.
  • Chanel, Inc. benefits and perks are dependent on eligibility and subject to modification by Chanel at any time

title: Designer
description:
Simon & Schuster was named to Forbes magazine list of America's Best Mid-Size Employers 2022. Simon & Schuster is a global leader in general interest publishing, dedicated to providing the best in fiction and nonfiction for readers of all ages, and in all printed, digital and audio formats. Its distinguished roster of authors includes many of the world's most popular and widely recognized writers, and winners of the most prestigious literary honors and awards. It is home to numerous well-known imprints and divisions such as Simon & Schuster, Scribner, Atria Books, Gallery Books, Pocket Books, Adams Media, Simon & Schuster Children's Publishing and Simon & Schuster Audio and international companies in Australia, Canada, India and the United Kingdom, and proudly brings the works of its authors to readers in more than 200 countries and territories. For more information visit our website at www.simonandschuster.com.

Simon & Schuster is seeking an Interior Book Designer to design and layout fiction and nonfiction books in the adult division. This person will be involved in, on average, 50 projects each year.

An Ideal Candidate

  • Is an Adobe InDesign expert and book design enthusiast
  • Is a talented designer with experience designing trade interiors
  • Is able to work at all levels, from inputting corrections to building well-structured InDesign files for their own original designs
  • Understands how their composition work impacts downstream processes, including ebook production and future print editions
  • Will work collaboratively with Production Editorial to ensure the manuscript and InDesign document are coded complementarily and coding best practices are applied rigorously from manuscript to finished InDesign files
  • Understands book production and printing standards
  • Knows the difference between match designs, reflows, conversions, reissues and is able to create all of these

Requirements

  • 2+ years of layout experience in Adobe InDesign; expert in InDesign
  • 1 year or more of experience designing original book interiors
  • Solid understanding of book composition standards
  • Ability to read and implement proofreaderâs marks
  • Experience manipulating illustrations and photos in Photoshop
  • Experience creating and manipulating vector art in Illustrator

This role is open to both hybrid and fully remote candidates.

Simon & Schuster US is an equal opportunity employer (EOE) including disability/vet. At Simon & Schuster US, the spirit of inclusion feeds into everything that we do. From employee benefits/programs and social impact outreach initiatives, we believe that opportunity, access, resources and rewards should be available to and for the benefit of all. Simon & Schuster US is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, and Veteran status.

The hiring salary range for this position applies to New York City, California, Colorado, Washington state, and most other geographies. Starting pay for the successful applicant depends on a variety of job-related factors, including but not limited to geographic location, market demands, experience, training, and education. The benefits available for this position include medical, dental, vision, 401(k) plan, life insurance coverage, disability benefits, tuition assistance program and PTO or, if applicable. Additionally, employees have access to our industry-first, Broad Based Ownership program, which makes all employees partners in our shared success.

Candidates hired for this or any other posted Simon & Schuster role will be employees of Simon & Schuster, LLC, subject to all policies, including the Workplace Privacy Notice, and eligible solely for the benefits plans thereof.

title: DevOps Engineer / System Engineer Linux & Data (m/w/d)
description:
Standort: Region Duisburg / Hybrid-Option

Anstellungsart: Vollzeit

Bereich: IT / System Engineering & Operations

Tech Stack: Linux, PostgreSQL, Docker, Kubernetes, Proxmox, ETL/ELT, Data Engineering

Für ein modernes, zukunftsorientiertes Technologieunternehmen wird zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt Verstärkung gesucht.

Das Unternehmen entwickelt maßgeschneiderte Softwareanwendungen, Datenstrukturen und zukunftsfähige IT-Infrastrukturen und bietet ein abwechslungsreiches Aufgabengebiet an der Schnittstelle von modernem Systembetrieb, Virtualisierung und zukunftsweisenden Datenlösungen.

Aufgaben

  • Infrastruktur & Plattformen: Du betreibst, wartest und entwickelst die internen IT-Systeme, Applikationsserver und produktiven Plattformen kontinuierlich weiter.
  • Linux & Core Administration: Die Administration von Linux-Systemen, relationalen Datenbanken (z.B. PostgreSQL), Backup-Konzepten sowie Monitoring- und Deployment-Lösungen liegt in deiner Hand.
  • Netzwerke & Virtualisierung: Du baust spezialisierte Netzwerke auf (inkl. Virtualisierung), übernimmst die Systemwartung und verantwortest die Migration sowie Betreuung kundenspezifischer Anwendungen.
  • Hardware & IT-Security: Du betreust, modernisierst und beschaffst Anlagen- und Auswerterechner unter Berücksichtigung aktueller Sicherheits- und Compliance-Anforderungen.
  • Data Engineering & Analytics: Du entwickelst und betreibst Data-Engineering-Lösungen, ETL/ELT-Pipelines und Datenbankstrukturen für den reibungslosen Betrieb von modernen Analytics- und KI-Anwendungen.

Qualifikation

  • Ausbildung: Eine abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung als Fachinformatiker für Systemintegration, Anwendungsentwicklung oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation im IT-Umfeld.
  • Erfahrung: Mehrjährige, einschlägige Berufserfahrung in der Administration und dem Engineering komplexer IT-Infrastrukturen.Deep Tech Skills: Sehr gute Linux-Kenntnisse sowie fundierte Erfahrung mit Virtualisierung (z.B. Docker, Kubernetes, Proxmox).
  • Daten & Monitoring: Sichere Kenntnisse im Betrieb von Datenbanken (PostgreSQL) und dem Aufsetzen moderner Monitoring-Systeme.
  • Persönlichkeit: Eine strukturierte, selbstständige Arbeitsweise, hohes Qualitäts- und Verantwortungsbewusstsein sowie ausgeprägte Teamfähigkeit und Kundenorientierung.
  • Sprachen: Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse für die interdisziplinäre Zusammenarbeit.

Benefits

  • Work-Life-Balance im Fokus: Fexible Gleitzeit und mindestens 30 Tagen Urlaub.
  • Finanzielle Sicherheit: Attraktive Vergütung inklusive Sonderzahlungen, starker betrieblicher Altersvorsorge und umfangreichen Sozialleistungen
  • Sinnstiftende Arbeit: Du arbeitest an zukunftsorientierten, nachhaltigen Projekten und leistest einen echten Beitrag zu modernen Technologielösungen.
  • Spannendes Tech-Umfeld: Abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben an der Schnittstelle von Data Engineering, DevOps und Systembetrieb für moderne Software- und KI-Anwendungen.
  • Moderne Führungskultur: Vertrauen, aktive Förderung, systematische Weiterbildungsprogramme und eine Fehlerkultur, die Raum zum Lernen lässt.
  • Klima & Kultur: Wertschätzendes Networking, interdisziplinäre Teams, modernstes Equipment sowie regelmäßige Teamevents.
  • Top Standort: Arbeiten in der Metropolregion Rhein-Ruhr mit hervorragender Verkehrsanbindung und vielseitigen Freizeitangeboten.

Bereit für den nächsten Karriereschritt?

Klicke einfach auf "Jetzt bewerben" und lade deinen Lebenslauf hoch.

title: B2B Marketing Manager (m/w/x)
description:
Deine Aufgaben

Du machst aus Content messbaren Impact. Mit starken Stories, datengetriebenen Kampagnen und smartem CRM-Einsatz sorgst du dafür, dass Bring! Labs sichtbar wächst, qualifizierte Leads gewinnt und langfristige Beziehungen aufbaut – entlang der gesamten Customer Journey. Dafür suchen wir, im Kontext einer Mutterschaftsvertretung, und idealerweise ab September 2026 bis Ende Januar 2028, eine:n B2B Marketing Manager (m/w/x) mit Fokus auf Content & Growth.

Was dich erwartet

In dieser Rolle verantwortest du Content und Lead-Generierung mit klarem Fokus auf die FMCG- und Retail-Branche im DACH-Raum. Du setzt hochwertige Inhalte strategisch ein, um qualifizierte Kontakte zu gewinnen und durch CRM-gestützte Maßnahmen weiterzuentwickeln.

  • Content & Formate
    • Erstellung von datenbasierten Branchen-Reports, Success Stories, Blogbeiträgen und LinkedIn-Posts
    • Konzeption zielgruppenspezifischer Inhalte entlang der gesamten Customer Journey
  • Lead-Generierung & Kampagnen
    • Aufbau und Optimierung von Inbound-, Automation- und Account-Based-Marketing-Strecken in enger Zusammenarbeit mit dem Sales-Team
    • Planung und Umsetzung von E-Mail-Marketing & Newslettern
    • Umsetzung und Steuerung von Kampagnen in HubSpot
  • Performance & Optimierung
    • Analyse, Tracking und kontinuierliche Optimierung aller Maßnahmen entlang relevanter KPIs

Dein Profil

Bei Bring! Labs denken wir groß – und suchen Menschen, die das mit Eigeninitiative, strukturierter Arbeitsweise und echtem Teamgeist mitgestalten wollen. Wir freuen uns darüber, wenn du Teil unseres Teams werden willst, und Folgendes mitbringst:

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium oder Ausbildung in Marketing, Kommunikation, Wirtschaft oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
  • Mindestens 5 Jahre Erfahrung im Content Marketing, Copywriting oder B2B-(Fach-)Journalismus
  • Nachweisbare Erfolge in der Lead-Generierung und Reaktivierung von Kontakten
  • Starkes Verständnis für KPIs, Data Storytelling und Datenvisualisierung
  • Fundierte HubSpot-Kenntnisse (Marketing & Content Hub, Workflows, Sequenzen) oder vergleichbares CRM
  • Routinierter Umgang mit KI-Tools für Recherche, Strukturierung und Effizienzsteigerung
  • Kenntnisse der Media-Branche oder Erfahrung im Digital Marketing von Handel und Marken von Vorteil
  • Deutsch auf Muttersprachniveau, sehr gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift

Warum wir?

Wir sind ein Tech-Unternehmen mit der Vision, die Kommunikation zwischen Marken und Konsument:innen zu revolutionieren. Bei Bring! Labs legen wir Wert auf eine professionelle und offene Arbeitskultur, in der unterschiedliche Perspektiven und Hintergründe willkommen sind. Wir bieten dir einen großartigen Arbeitsplatz, an dem du direkten Impact haben und deine Karriere weiterentwickeln kannst. Weiter bieten wir:

  • Ein attraktives und modernes Arbeitsumfeld im Herzen von Berlin, inklusive kostenlosem Barista-Grade Kaffee
  • Für deine Arbeit bekommst du das neuste MacBook/HP, inklusive Maus, Tastatur und hochwertigen Kopfhörern
  • Dein Geburtstag wird bei uns groß geschrieben. Daher hast du, ergänzend zu den 25 Tagen Urlaub, an deinem Geburtstag einen zusätzlichen freien Tag
  • Genieße die Flexibilität des hybriden Arbeitens: Treffe deine Kollegen in unserem Büro oder arbeite gelegentlich von zu Hause aus
  • Umweltfreundliche Reisemöglichkeiten, inklusive einer BahnCard 50
  • Wir lieben Social-Events: Alle 6 Monate treffen wir uns als gesamte Company in Zürich für ein geselliges Beisammensein. Die Reise inkl. Übernachtung wird selbstverständlich von Bring! Labs übernommen!

Klingt nach dir? Dann bewirb dich jetzt und werde Teil unseres Teams! Wir freuen uns auf frischen Wind, smarte Ideen und deinen Einsatz bei uns!
Willst du mehr wissen? Dann besuch unsere Karriereseite!

Wir berücksichtigen Bewerbungen unabhängig von Nationalität, ethnischem Hintergrund, Alter, sexueller Orientierung, Geschlecht, Behinderung oder familiärer Situation. Wir fördern auch die Bewerbungen von Berufsrückkehrern.

title: Strategic Account Executive (m/w/x)
description:
Deine Aufgaben

Bring! ist der meistgenutzte Einkaufsbegleiter im DACH-Raum und eine wachsende Werbeplattform für Retail- und FMCG-Marken. Als Strategic Account Executive im New Business bist du verantwortlich dafür, neue Werbepartner zu gewinnen: vom ersten Gespräch bis zur Unterschrift.

Du verkaufst kein Standardprodukt, sondern ein Medienformat, das Marken und Händler im entscheidenden Moment der Kaufentscheidung platziert. Hier setzt du an und vertreibst, problemzentriert und datengesteuert an C-Level und Marketing-Entscheider.

Deine ersten 90 Tage

  • Onboarding auf Produkt, Ad-Formate und unseren Sales-Prozess (SPICED / Winning by Design)
  • Eigenständige Übernahme einer bestehenden Liste relevanter Zielkunden
  • Ramp-Up und qualifizierter Pipeline Aufbau
  • Erste Deals in der Closing-Phase

Deine Rolle bei Bring! Labs:

Du gewinnst Marken für einen Werbekanal, den es so kein zweites Mal gibt:

  • Bring! erreicht Menschen nicht beim Scrollen, sondern im Moment der Einkaufsplanung direkt auf dem Einkaufszettel. Du bringst Retail- und FMCG-Marken in genau diesem Moment zu den Bring! User:innen.
  • Das heißt: Du verkaufst kein austauschbares Banner-Inventar, sondern einen Kanal mit klarem Performance-Versprechen. Deine Aufgabe ist es, das den richtigen Entscheidern verständlich zu machen.

Du baust dir deinen Markt selbst:

  • Du identifizierst, qualifizierst und gewinnst neue Werbepartner, über Outbound, eigene Recherche und B2B-Inbound.
  • Du navigierst durch komplexe Entscheidungsstrukturen bis dorthin, wo das Budget freigegeben wird: C-Level in Marketing, Media und Category.

Du gewinnst Deals, die zählen und gibst sie sauber weiter:

  • Du führst den kompletten Zyklus von der ersten Discovery bis zur Unterschrift, mit Fokus auf Deals mit echtem Hebel. Du nutzt SPICED nicht als Pflichtprozess, sondern um schneller zum eigentlichen Bedarf zu kommen.
  • Was du gewinnst, übergibst du strukturiert ans Account Management. Dein Erfolg endet nicht beim Vertragsabschluss, sondern bei einem Kunden, der bleibt.

Du machst deine Zahlen vorhersagbar:

  • Du übertriffst deine Monats- und Quartalsziele und deine Pipeline in HubSpot ist sauber genug, dass das niemanden überrascht.
  • Forecast-Hygiene ist für dich kein Reporting-Zwang, sondern die Grundlage dafür, dass du planbar lieferst.

Du gestaltest das Produkt mit:

  • Du bist am Markt näher dran als jede interne Funktion.
  • Dein Feedback an Marketing, Product und Sales Ops fließt direkt in unsere Ad-Formate und unser Pricing ein — du verkaufst nicht nur, du formst mit, was du verkaufst.

Dein Profil

description:

AI-Agent.

Wir entwickeln keine klassische Compliance-Software. Wir bauen eine Plattform, die Unternehmen dabei unterstützt, Governance dauerhaft steuerbar zu machen. Unsere Kunden sind mittelständische Unternehmen und internationale Konzerne.

title: Senior Business Development Director (f/m/d)
description:
Clariness is looking for a Senior Business Development Director (f/md/) based in Germany, UK, US, Serbia, South Africa.
- Full-time; unlimited -

 

About the role: 

Clariness helps patients access clinical trials faster through our ClinLife® platform, active in 50+ countries. In this role, you will turn expertise into real-world impact, connecting patients with studies and accelerating medical breakthroughs. Make your next role matter - help more patients join life-changing studies. 


As a Senior Business Development Director, you would be responsible for:

  • Prospect, develop, and close new business opportunities within the biotechnology, pharmaceutical, medical device, and CRO sectors.
  • Grow and manage strategic and existing customer accounts.
  • Build strong relationships with clients, partners, distributors, and key opinion leaders.
  • Represent the company at conferences, networking events, and client meetings.
  • Maintain an accurate sales pipeline and provide reliable sales forecasts.
  • Support leadership in achieving commercial objectives and identifying opportunities to improve products, services, and processes.
  • Act as a trusted advisor by bringing market insights and challenging existing approaches to drive continuous improvement.

We would like you to have: 

  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Life Sciences, Marketing, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.
  • Minimum 8 years of sales and business development experience with responsibility for achieving commercial targets.
  • Proven experience working with large pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology, CROs, or the broader life sciences industry.
  • Experience across key commercial and marketing disciplines, including communications, demand generation, and product marketing, in international markets.
  • Experience supporting or leading large-scale patient recruitment campaigns.
  • Demonstrated success in building strategic client relationships and driving business growth.
  • Strong communication, negotiation, presentation, and stakeholder management skills.
  • Experience hiring, mentoring, and developing high-performing commercial or marketing talent is an advantage.
  • Self-driven, strategic, and customer-focused with the ability to build lasting partnerships.

You may ask now, why should I work for you? Let us give you a few reasons – and you'll learn more during the process.

  • A varied and exciting job with a lot of personal responsibility in an international company, where you can develop and expand your skills.
  • Professional and personal development opportunities - incl. personal development budget.
  • Flexible working hours and mobile working.
  • Regular team events and open feedback culture.
  • A versatile field of activity and challenging projects.
  • A supportive and open company culture, providing the opportunity to collaborate with a diverse and professional team.
  • Impact healthcare by accelerating medical innovation through improved access to clinical trials, potentially bringing needed treatments to patients faster.

 

How to apply & what to expect: 

  • Apply via link (CV only; no cover letter required).
  • Introductory call (30 min) → Technical interview → Senior Leadership Interview →  Demo Day → Decision.
  • We aim to move promptly and keep you updated at every step. 

 

At Clariness, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees, regardless of race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, disability or age.

For more information, feel free to reach out to

Have we sparked your interest?

title: Junior Security Operations Engineering Consultant (m/w/d)
description:
It all starts with the mission: NVISO is here to protect European society from potentially devastating cyber attacks! This means we offer cyber security services to private and governmental organizations to help them better prepare for, prevent, detect and respond to cyber security incidents.

All of this is built on four fundamental values that define who we are: We are Proud, We Break Barriers, We Care and No BS!

Tasks

As a Junior Security Operations Engineering Consultant (m/w/d) located in Germany you will assist in various Security Operations Center (SOC) projects. You will gain exposure to SOC engineering concepts such as log onboarding, detection engineering, and SIEM technologies. Under the guidance of senior team members, you will support the design and implementation of security solutions for customers across different sectors.

You have strong communication and interpersonal skills, which enable you not only to understand requirements, but also to put these requirements into a development plan, explain it to customers and guarantee proper execution. You have an open and approachable mind, in line with NVISO's values.

Typical tasks include but are not limited to:

  • Planning and managing the onboarding of new log sources, troubleshooting issues with log sources or systems with clients and vendors;
  • Creating technical and process playbooks;
  • Defining and setting up correlation rules, based on threat intelligence and specific customer requirements;
  • Contributing to incident response procedures;
  • Ensuring you remain up to speed with latest trends and technologies in the SOC field.

You are passionate about cyber security, engineering and automation. Building solutions and working with customers energizes you and you look forward to growing in all the aforementioned domains.

Requirements

  • You hold citizenship in one of the 32 NATO member states or the Austrian citizenship;
  • 1-2 years of practical experience (such as a SOC Analyst) in the field of cybersecurity or IT is mandatory;
  • Experience with Azure Sentinel, Microsoft Security stack, or SIEM technologies such as Elastic, Splunk, or LogPoint;
  • Experience with scripting in Python and/or other relevant scripting or automation language is a big plus;
  • Ideally, familiarity with SIEM query languages or an interest in learning Sigma and YARA rules;
  • Experience or coursework related to cybersecurity is beneficial.

Soft skills

  • Ability to work independently, and keep track of your priorities;
  • Strong interpersonal and verbal/written communication skills that enable the ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment across the entire company;
  • Excellent English and German communication skills, both verbal and written;
  • A positive, team-oriented, and mission-driven attitude;
  • Ability to prepare and present your work to colleagues.

Benefits

At NVISO, we care. We are committed to offering you a highly competitive remuneration package including financial and non-financial components:

  • Working and learning from the best people in the European cyber security industry. We have multiple SANS Instructors working at NVISO, our staff has presented at popular hacking conferences (BlackHat, BruCON, OWASP, etc) and all of our technical staff can acquire deep technical security certifications (GSE, GXPN, GREM, GCFA, OSCP, etc);
  • Generous training budget of 10.000 EUR + 10 man days for attending lectures rolling over 2 years;
  • An annual gross base salary between 57.000 EUR and 62.000 EUR, depending on your experience;
  • Support for technical growth with Cloud trainings + certifications (AWS, GCP, Azure);
  • Regular team-building and fun events;
  • Our commitment to coach and counsel you and help you grow; each employee receives a personal coach within the team, whose role is to ensure your well-being and helps you grow in your career!
  • Flexible working hours and home office possibilities (incl. working abroad weeks within the EU);
  • Business Bike Leasing;
  • BahnCard 50 1st class + public transfer ticket;
  • 30 holidays;
  • Cool offices in the center of Frankfurt, Munich and Vienna (with BBQ, kicker table, table tennis, playstations, etc.).

Disclaimer on the Use of AI Tools in the Application Process

Please be aware that the creation and submission of application documents (e.g. CV, cover letter, case studies, etc.) using AI-powered tools is only permitted to a limited extent.

Our expectations:

Application documents must authentically reflect your own qualifications, personality, and motivation.

The use of AI for supportive purposes (e.g. spell-checking, improving wording) is acceptable.

Fully generated application documents created by AI without personal adaptation or review are not permitted.

Under no circumstances may NVISO information, data, or documents be uploaded to or processed by external AI tools.

We reserve the right to exclude applications from the selection and interview process that are clearly created primarily or exclusively by AI and show no recognizable personal input.

The purpose of this policy is to ensure a fair and transparent recruitment process and to obtain an authentic impression of our applicants.

title: Solutions Architect - Enterprise Architecture IT, Industrial Automation & Transformation
description:
Salary Transparency:

This position is intended for candidates with more than 7 years of professional experience.

The salary range varies depending on experience, between €120,000 – €150,000 (OTE).

Work model: Full-time, fully remote within Germany, with onboarding at the Dresden head office and occasional in-person events.

Location: Germany, preferably in the Rhine-Main, Berlin, Munich, Düsseldorf, or Cologne/Bonn regions.

Muxon is a small, globally active recruitment agency and is conducting this search on behalf of our partner, an innovative industrial robotics company operating at the intersection of Physical AI and software-defined automation. We are looking for an experienced Solutions Architect who can translate complex business needs into scalable IT/OT architecture strategies and support enterprise customers in their automation journey.

Aufgaben

  • Translate customer business goals into platform value narratives, architecture principles, and adoption strategies.
  • Co-design target IT–OT architectures for software-defined automation and Physical AI across multi-site environments.
  • Lead maturity assessments and architecture workshops with business, IT, and OT stakeholders.
  • Build phased transformation roadmaps with clear risk mitigation and value realization plans.
  • Support Sales in executive-level conversations, business cases, and long-term framework agreements.
  • Identify expansion opportunities across sites, use cases, and regions.
  • Ensure architectural continuity across the full customer lifecycle.

Qualifikation

  • 8+ years of experience as an Enterprise Architect, Solution Architect, or IT/OT Transformation Architect.
  • Proven experience in large enterprise environments and automation domains.
  • Strong understanding of cloud and enterprise IT architectures (OT experience is not required).
  • Confidence working with C-level stakeholders.
  • Excellent workshop facilitation, structuring, and abstraction skills
  • Business-outcome driven mindset with ownership, curiosity, and pragmatism.
  • Fluent English and German at C1 level.

Benefits

  • High strategic influence on enterprise architecture decisions.
  • A unique opportunity to shape a future-facing field: Physical AI and software-defined automation.
  • 50% of remote work within Germany, with strong flexibility and autonomy.
  • A high-impact role with direct visibility across business, IT, and OT.

If this challenge sounds like the right next step for your career, we encourage you to apply and join a team shaping the future of industrial automation.
